summary: Open-side graph B integration attempt: re-sync B10 sources, occupancy adapter via CellWidths (un-abbreviated widths), corpus + live gates, swap or keep-and-report

result.gate:
Everything is confirmed and consistent. License `--check` = **0 stale**, exit 0. The three re-synced files (doclayout, generate, pretty) are untracked campaign files I updated to round-10; dot/mod/model were already at round-10 (I did not touch them). No commits, no HS servers started, no sealed-workspace sources modified. Task complete.

Here is the plain-text summary (my return value):

---

GRAPH ROUND-10 OCCUPANCY-ADAPTER INTEGRATION — open side, 2026-07-18

RE-SYNC (step 1). Round-10 graph-clean sources vendored into `/home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/crates/tamarin-server/src/graph_clean/`. Only three files changed vs the round-8 copy — `doclayout.rs`, `generate.rs`, `pretty.rs` (the round-9/10 fill engine + the NEW `CellWidths`/`group_widths_with`/`RawRule::{premise,conclusion}_widths` surface); the other eight are byte-stable. Mechanical `crate::`→`super::` only; reverse-transform diffs the workspace with zero content lines (only the pre-existing test `use super::*;`). Serializer roundtrip 12022/12022 byte-exact; workspace suite green incl. both override regression tests; `tamarin-server` lib 111 + all route suites green; `gen_license_headers.py --check` = 0 stale (the three files are headerless and carry no resolving `.hs` citation — the only token, `HughesPJ.hs`, is the BSD library, not a submodule file).

ADAPTER DESIGN (two sentences). For every prem/concl record cell the adapter recovers the reference's internal (UN-abbreviated) rendering by fixpoint-expanding the graph's own legend `NAME = EXPANSION` map back into the display text, then computes the round-10 shape law on that internal text (occupancy = flat_int + Σ tuple/union(elems+1); fill_width = flat_int + Σ union(elems+1) + #function-nodes; bonus = max ⌊elems/2⌋+2) and supplies it as a per-cell `generate::CellWidths` override through `group_widths_with` / `RawRule::*_widths`. It is an open-side measurement harness (`/tmp/claude-1000/-home-kamilner-tamarin-rs/57f8e1bc-d495-4b36-93c0-033347d5034a/scratchpad/occ_census/`) that computes INPUTS only — no render logic left the clean module, and a cell with no abbreviation gets the display-text default byte-for-byte.

GATE NUMBERS (step 3, full 12022-payload GRAPHCLEAN_CORPUS gate; before → after occupancies):
- ALL prem/concl cells: 594789/615850 = 96.580%  →  occ-only 589526/615850 = 95.726%  (occ+bonus+fill 95.650%)
- WRAP-SENSITIVE (wrapping cells): 122957/142540 = 86.261%  →  occ-only 119579/142540 = 83.892%  (occ+bonus+fill 83.566%)
- multi-cell wrapping: 85.291% → 82.714%;  abbreviated-group wrapping: 83.746% → 73.577%
- Per-cell lever delta (occ-only vs baseline): FIXED 372 (117 abbr) / BROKEN 5635 (2350 abbr), net −5263.
- Root cause isolated: the shipped display-flat TRIGGER is already 99.627% accurate overall and 99.459% on abbreviated groups; the symmetric internal-width trigger is WORSE (98.445% / 88.405%). Charging siblings their full internal width over-tightens budgets (false-positives 1478→3363, the reference's coupled-`fits` relief), the cell's own trigger has no self-width override (lone-abbreviated false-negatives unclosable), and `wrap_cell_dot` lays out post-abbreviation text so abbreviated cells' break positions are unreproducible at any budget.

Supplying the internal widths REGRESSES the gate; the adapter is therefore NOT usable as-is.

ADOPTION OUTCOME (step 4): NOT PERFORMED — keep-and-report. No byte-green surface, full or subset: the occupancy adapter regresses; even the no-override baseline reproduces only 89.828% of records all-cells-exact and 86.261% of wrapping cells (the ported `handlers/dot::render_balanced` is byte-faithful to HS on exactly these, so any route through clean silently regresses ~10% of records); and, unchanged from rounds 2–8, the clean serializer emits the HS dialect (`digraph "G" {`, global `<n_k>` ports) while the only byte-sensitive graph test pins the ported dialect and the repo has no HS graph byte oracle. Kept intact (headers untouched): `handlers/dot.rs`, `graph/{a
